svg.text: The atomicity race has two distinct natures. Each motivates a different fix.

text.tspan: The intent is on disk on every node the reader touches. Per-key visibility
hinges on the resolver's view of the txn record's status, which depends on
when the call physically lands relative to SetTxnStatus.
Two calls in one read can disagree: one sees ACTIVE, the other COMMITTED.
snapshot_ts filters by version_ts, not by status — it cannot pin
the resolver's view of the txn record.
Closed by: force the writer to decide before the call returns
The reader's per-key call lands on a node where the write hasn't physically
arrived. Only the prior version is on disk; the call returns the prior value.
Meanwhile, on a different node, the same transaction's intent has arrived
and committed — its key returns the new value.
snapshot_ts cannot filter in writes that haven't landed; push-the-writer
has nothing on this node to push against.
Closed by: pin the snapshot to a globally-settled instant
